codeReview.summary: The fix resolves the original blocker. `src/pipeline/daily.py` now routes default live inference through `build_live_feature_frame()`, and `src/model/data_builder.py` rebuilds same-day offense, pitching, defense, bullpen, baseline, and weather-backed inference inputs from current schedule plus historical context instead of reusing persisted feature rows. The new regression tests explicitly prove stale feature rows are ignored and the pipeline builder consumes the fresh live-assembly path. A pre-existing non-blocking odds snapshot duplication issue remains in the daily pipeline.

issues.description: `run_daily_pipeline()` still calls `_persist_odds_snapshots()` after `odds_fetcher()` returns, but the default prod fetcher delegates to `fetch_mlb_odds()` which already persists the same snapshots inside `src/clients/odds_client.py`. Production runs will continue to double-write each fetched odds row until one of those persistence paths is removed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `.factory/library/workspace-state.md` is now stale for t24. It still says the live pipeline intentionally reuses persisted feature rows and skips full same-day recomputation, but this fix switched the default pipeline path back to fresh same-day feature assembly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/workspace-state.md:39 says the pipeline reuses persisted rows; current code in src/pipeline/daily.py:498-516 routes to `build_live_feature_frame()`, and src/model/data_builder.py:332-363 rebuilds live same-day features in a temporary database before assembling inference rows.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared Windows bootstrap command remains unreliable. The worker had to deviate from the documented init flow because the manifest's `init_windows` inline `init_db` command is misquoted, so shared setup guidance and the executable command source are still out of sync.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:7 contains `init_db(''data/mlb.db'')`, and the fix handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-20T05-41-33-453Z__fix-t24-live-features__8429612f-eef5-4b12-a99e-2ac192cdaef6.json` records a justified deviation because the manifest command failed on PowerShell.

summary: Pass. The fix fully addresses the prior blocking review finding by replacing stale/default live inference inputs with fresh same-day feature assembly and adding regressions that guard against stale-row reuse. The only remaining issue I found is the pre-existing non-blocking double-persistence of odds snapshots in the pipeline.
